danger_signals.visual_form: Ground-based turret installations visible on overhead map
danger_signals.meaning: Immediate threat to helicopter; avoid or destroy before engagement

danger_signals.visual_form: Mobile enemy units rendered in 2.5D overhead perspective
danger_signals.meaning: Ground-based hostile forces requiring evasion or destruction

danger_signals.visual_form: Helicopter damage state (visual degradation or HUD bar)
danger_signals.meaning: Player vulnerability increasing; evasion or repair needed

danger_signals.visual_form: Fuel gauge on HUD; vehicle becomes unstable
danger_signals.meaning: Imminent crash if not refueled; critical resource scarcity

danger_signals.visual_form: Opening cinematic shows nuclear detonation on deserted island
danger_signals.meaning: Existential threat; establishes world-state as under attack

safety_signals.meaning: Safe location for hostage delivery and mission completion

safety_signals.visual_form: Collectible objects (armor replenishment, ammo crates, fuel barrels)
safety_signals.meaning: Resource restoration; temporary relief from resource pressure
Armor replenishment upon delivery; ally presence increases safety

safety_signals.visual_form: Fuel barrel objects scattered in level
safety_signals.meaning: Extended operational duration; continued mission viability

faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Washington D.C. locations; presidential structures; friendly landing zones

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Nuclear weapons; desert island hideout symbolism
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Terrorist soldiers; anti-aircraft installations; armed vehicles

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Cartel soldiers; armoured cars; drug-related installations
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Jungle terrain; cocaine production facilities; cartel hideout progression

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Commander Ptofski; oil tankers; Siberian/Alaskan locations
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Alaska; oil tankers; arctic/cold environment aesthetic

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Active terrorist threat to Washington D.C.
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Opening level depicts attack on President's limousine; urban environment with government buildings under siege
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player must repel attacks in real-time; enemy forces actively engage civilian/government targets

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Nuclear explosion in opening cinematic; references to 'nuclear resources' delivery; final level trucks carrying nuclear bombs
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player must prevent truck convoy from reaching White House; time-pressure mechanic tied to nuclear threat

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Mid-game progression moves to jungle terrain; fortress structures visible in overhead perspective; dense vegetation replacing urban environments
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemy density increases; cartel forces defend territorial positions; player advances through operational stages

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Final level returns to Washington D.C.; visible vehicle convoys fleeing
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Chase sequence mechanic; player pursues both Kilbaba and Ortega in climactic confrontation

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Alaska level; oil tankers positioned as environmental weapons; arctic landscape
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player must eliminate Ptofski's forces before oil spill occurs; environmental destruction mechanic

environmental_storytelling.example: Campaign moves from Washington D.C. (homeland defense) → jungle locations (offensive operations) → fortress assault (climactic confrontation) → D.C. finale (restored order). Each geography visually communicates escalation and mission progression.

environmental_storytelling.technique: Enemy installation density signals territorial control
environmental_storytelling.example: Jungle fortress region contains concentrated anti-aircraft guns, armoured vehicles, and fortified positions. Visual clustering of enemy assets communicates power concentration and player challenge scaling.

Rescue missions require airlifting friendly personnel to landing zones. Visual representation of human assets tied to armor replenishment creates mechanical/narrative link between compassion and survival.

F-117 stealth jet pickup offers temporary power escalation (unlimited ammo, variable height) but increased vulnerability. Visual transition from helicopter to jet communicates risk-reward tactical choice.
Fuel gauges, ammo indicators, and armor bars create persistent visual anxiety. Environmental placement of fuel barrels and ammo crates creates resource-hunt gameplay loop visible on overhead map.

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Maneuvers freely; can engage multiple enemies; mission progress accelerates
Reduced maneuverability; evasion prioritized; defensive gameplay required
Flight destabilization increases; crash risk imminent; must seek fuel pickups or landing zone
Track and fire upon player helicopter; create no-fly zones; force tactical routing
Cluster around fortified positions; increase density near primary objectives; defend fuel/ammo supplies
Player gains armor upon landing; friendly presence signals temporary safety
Final level vehicle pursuit; fleeing Washington D.C.; create chase-sequence climax mechanic

notes: Jungle Strike uses a 2.5D overhead perspective to communicate tactical space while maintaining action-game intensity. The visual language emphasizes resource management (fuel, ammo, armor) as core world-state indicators. Faction identity is communicated primarily through environmental context (geographic setting, installation type) rather than color-coding or heraldic symbols. The game's political narrative (US special forces vs. terrorist/cartel conspiracy) is established through opening cinematic and level progression geography, with visual design supporting the campaign's escalation from domestic defense to foreign offensive operations. Power-up placement creates implicit 'difficulty zones' — dangerous areas guarded by supplies signal player resource pressure points.
